channel.title: NPR Topics: World
channel.description: NPR world news, international art and culture, world business and financial markets, world economy, and global trends in health, science and technology. Subscribe to the World Story of the Day podcast and RSS feed.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Bulgarian banger 'Bangaranga' bags country its 1st Eurovision win
item.description: Bulgaria was not among the favorites to win. But performer Dara's catchy, banging anthem bested 24 other nations at the 70th edition of the international singing extravaganza.
item.content:encoded:
Bulgaria was not among the favorites to win. But performer Dara's catchy, banging anthem bested 24 other nations at the 70th edition of the international singing extravaganza.

(Image credit: Helmut Fohringer)
item.dc:creator: Chloe Veltman

item.title: Iranian man barely survives bombing, but wants war to go on
item.description: An Iranian man who was buried under rubble in US Israel bombing campaign says he wants the war to go on, and destroy the regime.
item.content:encoded: An Iranian man who was buried under rubble in US Israel bombing campaign says he wants the war to go on, and destroy the regime.
item.dc:creator: Durrie Bouscaren

item.title: Life on the edge: why Indonesians living near volcanoes won't leave
item.description: Indonesia has more than 120 active volcanoes. On the island of Java, thousands live alongside Mount Merapi, constantly balancing risks -- and most say they won't leave.
item.content:encoded: Indonesia has more than 120 active volcanoes. On the island of Java, thousands live alongside Mount Merapi, constantly balancing risks -- and most say they won't leave.
item.dc:creator: Naina Rao

item.title: Timmy the humpback whale found dead off Danish coast
item.description: A humpback whale found dead this week off a Danish island has been identified as the animal released two weeks ago in a spectacular and controversial rescue effort after repeatedly becoming stranded off Germany's Baltic Sea coast, Danish authorities said Saturday.
item.content:encoded:
A humpback whale found dead this week off a Danish island has been identified as the animal released two weeks ago in a spectacular and controversial rescue effort after repeatedly becoming stranded off Germany's Baltic Sea coast, Danish authorities said Saturday.

(Image credit: Philip Dulian)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: The war in Ukraine is at a standstill as societal fatigue grows in Russia
item.description: With the peace process stalled, there are signs of growing public fatigue in Russia with the Kremlin's war as Ukraine uses robotic warfare to stay in the fight.
item.content:encoded: With the peace process stalled, there are signs of growing public fatigue in Russia with the Kremlin's war as Ukraine uses robotic warfare to stay in the fight.
item.dc:creator: Charles Maynes

item.title: The townspeople of Vilseck, Germany, worry that Trump may pull out 5,000 U.S. troops
item.description: President Trump's troop withdrawal threat rattles residents of a small Bavarian town reliant on U.S. military personnel and their families for both income and friendships built over decades.
item.content:encoded:
President Trump's troop withdrawal threat rattles residents of a small Bavarian town reliant on U.S. military personnel and their families for both income and friendships built over decades.

(Image credit: Lisi Niesner)
item.dc:creator: Rob Schmitz

item.title: Trump says Islamic State group leader was killed in a joint U.S.-Nigerian mission
item.description: Trump announced the joint operation in Africa's most populous country in a late-night social media post. He said Abu Bakr al-Mainuki was second in command of the Islamic State group globally.
item.content:encoded:
Trump announced the joint operation in Africa's most populous country in a late-night social media post. He said Abu Bakr al-Mainuki was second in command of the Islamic State group globally.

(Image credit: Mark Schiefelbein)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: The Eurovision Song Contest reaches its grand final with pop and protests
item.description: The final of the Eurovision Contest arrives Saturday, with tight security and rainy weather failing to dent the enthusiasm of fans, or the opposition of critics who think Israel shouldn't be invited.
item.content:encoded:
The final of the Eurovision Contest arrives Saturday, with tight security and rainy weather failing to dent the enthusiasm of fans, or the opposition of critics who think Israel shouldn't be invited.

(Image credit: Martin Meissner)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Israel says it killed the leader of Hamas' military wing
item.description: Israel says it has killed the leader of Hamas' military wing, one of the architects of the Oct, 7, 2023, attacks that triggered the war in Gaza.
item.content:encoded:
Israel says it has killed the leader of Hamas' military wing, one of the architects of the Oct, 7, 2023, attacks that triggered the war in Gaza.

(Image credit: Jehad Alshrafi)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Iran has been confiscating property from critics of the regime
item.description: Iran's government has been confiscating property from people it deems traitors or critics of the regime.
item.content:encoded: Iran's government has been confiscating property from people it deems traitors or critics of the regime.
item.dc:creator: Emily Feng
